title: kpod exec
body:
Initial wiring of kpod exec. We wont support the following options
for exec:

* detach -- unsure of use case
* detach-keys -- not supported by runc
* interactive -- all terminals will be interactive

Not adding exec tests as we need to think about how to support a
test that requires console access but our CI tests have no console.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: @rhatdan I need some help translating --privileged into cap-adds.  What does privileged mean in those terms.
As far as --privileged is concerned, I think you will need to generate a different bundle for that with SELinux/AppArmor/SECCOMP turned off and --all-caps turned on.
fixed that privileged cap-adds are only trigger when --privileged is used.
Fixed the random number comment.  Still unsure of testing and how that should work when no console is present.
@baude Can't you just exec into a running contianer a single command without a console?

LGTM
If @mheon gives a LGTM we can merge.
One question I'd like answered before we merge, otherwise looks fine
@jlebon Any idea why this is not working?
